output_boundaries.rule: Maintained output ./app/ is project-owned; agents may edit it directly after reading the relevant Topogram packets.

edit_boundaries.rule: Edit Topogram and policy files first. Avoid generated-owned output paths unless converting them to maintained ownership.

file_organization.parserRule: Folder layout is for humans and agents; Topogram flattens statements into one graph.

first_commands.reason: Check local CLI, package, and catalog setup.
See whether template-derived files diverged locally.
Understand whether the project is template-attached or detached.
Validate package-backed generator policy before generation.
Read current SDLC profile, enforcement mode, obligations, and protected paths.
Find unclaimed tasks, unresolved bugs, and approved requirements needing a task.
Read blockers, rules, decisions, proof gaps, and verification guidance before implementation.
Verify protected work has SDLC linkage before PR/CI.
Start SDLC-backed implementation from the current task or bug.
Validate Topogram, project config, topology, ownership, trust, and generator policy.
Read a focused UI/widget packet before UI work.
Write generated-owned runtime/app outputs after validation.

read_order.reason: Human-readable first-run guidance generated with this project.
Project workflow and template provenance summary.
Topology, outputs, template metadata, generator bindings, and implementation provider settings.
SDLC adoption, profile, enforcement mode, protected paths, required item kinds, and exemption policy.
Template trust/update policy for attached templates.
Package-backed generator policy and allowed scopes.
Executable implementation trust record, if the template copied implementation code.
Brownfield extraction provenance and source evidence, if this came from extract.
Canonical Topogram graph source. Use focused query packets for implementation work.

obligations: Protected changes need a valid SDLC item, an SDLC record change, or an allowed exemption.
Done tasks need requirement, acceptance, and verification proof links.
Optional stale/WIP policy can warn or fail through prep commit and gate.
profileDetails.summary: Standard profile keeps non-trivial work traceable without enterprise audit metadata.

outputs.rule: Maintained output ./app/ is project-owned; agents may edit it directly after reading the relevant Topogram packets.

warnings: SDLC is enforced. Protected changes need a valid SDLC item, a topo/sdlc/*.tg record update, or an explicit allowed exemption.
AGENTS.md is missing. Use this command as the current agent guidance source.

workflows.rule: Start from an SDLC task packet before implementation. Plans are optional; edit plan text directly, but use CLI for status, history, step progress, and archive state.
workflows.title: SDLC task and plan loop

workflows.rule: Edit the Topogram first, then regenerate generated-owned outputs.
workflows.title: Generated project loop

workflows.rule: Use focused widget and surface packets before editing UI code.
workflows.title: Widget-first UI loop

workflows.rule: Local Topogram files are project-owned after edits; review update plans before applying template changes.
workflows.title: Template update loop
